rulings.Case Title: MOLALLA HOLDINGS, INC. DBA vs. KARO, STEVEN IND/DBA PROPERTY MAINTENANCE SERVICE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec. And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 23, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF MOLALLA HOLDINGS, INC. DBA ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE MANAGEMENT SOLUTIONS' Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel GRANTED. No sanctions to issue. Moving party to prepare an order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN WILKINS VS. MARY BAYTOP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Sale Of Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 23, 2012, Line 2, Order To Show Cause Re: Sale Of Dwelling is Denied without prejudice to being refiled. Plaintiff failed to satisfy the mandatory requirements of Code of Civil Procedure to state the amount of each lien and encumbrance and the address of each person recording the lien and encumbrance. Moreover, because defendant is entitled to a homestead exemption of $175, 000, the fair market value of the property of $284,000 as set forth in the appraisal and the known liens (excluding the one recorded by plaintiff) total $74,827.07, the court cannot make a determination that the sale of the property would result in any payment to plaintiffAny party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 23, 2012, Line 28, DEFENDANT WWC SERVICES OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC. Notice Of Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens IS Denied. Plaintiff has shown that he has a probably valid real property claim by his causes of action for partition and corporate dissolution, Plaintiff substantially complied with the service and filing requirements and Defendant does not contend that it did not receive actual notice. rulings.Case Title: JERUSALEM CHURCH OF GOD IN CHRIST, A CALIFORNIA VS. TIMOTHY JONES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction & Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 23, 2012, Line 23, Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction & Temporary Restraining Order IS Denied. Plaintiff has not shown by preponderance of the evidence that Pastor Galbraith had the authority to terminate the board members and to remove them as members of the Church. Defendants dispute that plaintiff has such authority. The Constitution of the national Church expressly provides that a local church may establish its own rules not inconsistent with the rules of the national Church. Nothing in the Constitution precludes a local church from establishing rules that bar a pastor from removing board members or terminating their membership in the local church. Moreover, there is a legitimate dispute whether the board had the authority to terminate Pastor Galbraith. =302/HK
